prompt: Create a cinematic black-and-white fine-art photograph of a ghostly ballerina dancing alone inside an abandoned old theater or ballroom. The dancer is positioned slightly right of center, wearing a translucent knee-length ballet dress and pointe shoes, with her arms extended in an elegant, fragile pose; use a long-exposure multiple-exposure effect so her body appears as two or three overlapping semi-transparent silhouettes, suggesting motion and haunting presence. The room is dark, decayed, and atmospheric, with cracked plaster, an arched alcove or stage opening on the right, rows of shadowed seats or debris in the background, and tall broken arched windows on the left. Strong sunlight blasts through the window from the left rear, creating visible shafts of dusty light, smoky haze, high-contrast rim lighting, and a bright rectangular patch of light on the wooden floor beneath the dancer. Use dramatic backlight with volumetric dust beams, deep blacks, glowing highlights, film grain, soft blur from movement, and a moody gothic mood. Compose in a horizontal 4:3 frame, wide interior view, low-to-mid camera height, with the dancer small enough to show the environment. No text, no modern objects, no color, no clean studio look.
